mission.title: k0s: kube-vip support (load balancing + VIP)?
mission.description: kube-vip support (load balancing + VIP)?. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current k0s deployment
steps.description:
Verify your k0s version and configuration:
```bash
k0s version
k0s status
```
This feature requires a working k0s installation.

steps.title: Review k0s configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant k0s configuration:
```bash
k0s version
k0s kubectl get nodes -o wide
k0s kubectl get pods -A
```
Hi everyone,

I just tried to install kube-vip (load balancing + VIP) on k0s control planes, but it wasn't possible since the control planes are isolated. I also tried to install kube-vip on k0s worker nodes, but it didn't work as well.

steps.title: Apply the fix for kube-vip support (load balancing + VIP)?
steps.description:
you're right, I absolutely forgot that we propagate the custom port to the "clients" too automatically. Had to read our HA custom port test case for that. 😄 

So basically the only good option I see (apart from the client-side balancer thingy we're already working on) is to make the bind address
```yaml
frontend kubeAPI
    bind lb-ip:6443
    default_backend kubeAPI_backend
frontend konnectivity
    bind lb-ip:8132
    default_backend konnectivity_backend
frontend controllerJoinAPI
    bind lb-ip:9443
    default_backend controllerJoinAPI_backend
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
k0s kubectl get nodes -o wide
k0s kubectl get pods -A
k0s kubectl get events -A --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "kube-vip support (load balancing + VIP)?" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
you're right, I absolutely forgot that we propagate the custom port to the "clients" too automatically. Had to read our HA custom port test case for that. 😄 

So basically the only good option I see (apart from the client-side balancer thingy we're already working on) is to make the bind address configurable so we can bind all controlplane components to some non VIP address on the host.
codeSnippets:
frontend kubeAPI
    bind lb-ip:6443
    default_backend kubeAPI_backend
frontend konnectivity
    bind lb-ip:8132
    default_backend konnectivity_backend
frontend controllerJoinAPI
    bind lb-ip:9443
    default_backend controllerJoinAPI_backend

prerequisites.description: A running k0s cluster or a system where k0s can be installed.
